title: convert_existing_path does not expand ~ home directory
body:
convert_existing_path() in radicli/util.py calls Path(path_str).exists() without expanding the tilde. Paths like "~/myfile.txt" always fail even when the file exists.



Fix: call Path(path_str).expanduser() before the exists() check in convert_existing_path() and the related convert_existing_file_path() and convert_existing_dir_path() functions.
Add a test in radicli/tests/test_util.py using a tmp_path fixture.
